rubric_items.reasoning: All required resources match spec exactly (names, labels, ports, image, replica count, PVC size, insecure mode, reused RBAC). Agent correctly ran cockroach init to bootstrap the fresh cluster and verified readiness/endpoints/PVC binding before reporting success, plus added a PDB to satisfy the explicit quorum-preservation constraint. This is a durable, root-cause-correct deployment, not a lucky pass.

Visible log shows a sensible diagnose-wait-verify sequence: correctly interpreted a transient post-init 503/SQL-not-ready response as expected warm-up rather than failure, polled pod readiness in a bounded loop, then ran a full final verification sweep. A prior background rollout-status apparently timed out before init completed, but the agent correctly identified this as stale rather than backtracking or panicking, so it's a minor rough edge rather than real flailing.
All commands are targeted, namespace-scoped reads (get, exec curl for health) plus exactly the mutations needed. No broad, forceful, or out-of-scope tooling was used.

Only 4 mutations total, mapping 1:1 to the minimal required resource set (2 Services, 1 StatefulSet, 1 PDB). The PDB is not scope creep — it directly implements the stated quorum-preservation requirement. No CRDs, operators, or extraneous resources were introduced.
